pattern: Each position's answer is the product of everything to its left times everything to its right, both computable in single passes so no division is ever needed.
hints: The answer at index i is the product of the prefix before i and the suffix after i.
Accumulate running prefix products left to right, then multiply in running suffix products right to left.
First fill answer[i] with the product of all earlier elements, then sweep from the right multiplying by a running product of all later elements.

edge_inputs.description: a single zero zeroes every position but its own
two zeros force every output to zero

stress.description: random small-magnitude integers including zeros so prefix/suffix logic is stressed
stress.note: value range matches constraints so products stay 32-bit safe per problem guarantee

stress.description: no-zero arrays where every output is a genuine nonzero product
stress.note: avoids zeros to test the general nonzero path
